lifecycle.c(3) Library Functions Manual lifecycle.c(3)

NAME

lifecycle.c - Lock-free module lifecycle state machine implementation.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


bool lifecycle_init (lifecycle_t *lc, const char *name)
bool lifecycle_init_once (lifecycle_t *lc)
void lifecycle_init_commit (lifecycle_t *lc)
void lifecycle_init_abort (lifecycle_t *lc)
bool lifecycle_shutdown (lifecycle_t *lc)
bool lifecycle_shutdown_forever (lifecycle_t *lc)
bool lifecycle_is_initialized (const lifecycle_t *lc)
bool lifecycle_is_dead (const lifecycle_t *lc)
bool lifecycle_reset (lifecycle_t *lc)
bool lifecycle_destroy_once (lifecycle_t *lc)
void lifecycle_destroy_commit (lifecycle_t *lc)

Detailed Description

Lock-free module lifecycle state machine implementation.

Implements concurrent init/shutdown synchronization using atomic compare-and-swap. See include/ascii-chat/util/lifecycle.h for API documentation and usage patterns.

Definition in file lifecycle.c.

Function Documentation

void lifecycle_destroy_commit (lifecycle_t * lc)

Commit successful destruction: DESTROYING → UNINITIALIZED. Call this after lifecycle_destroy_once() returns true and destroy work completes.

Parameters

lc lifecycle state

Returns the module to UNINITIALIZED state, allowing future re-initialization.

Definition at line 224 of file lifecycle.c.

224                                                {
225   if (lc == NULL) {
226     log_dev("[lifecycle] destroy_commit: NULL lifecycle pointer");
227     return;
228   }
229   log_dev("[lifecycle] destroy_commit: transitioning DESTROYING → UNINITIALIZED");
230   atomic_store_u64(&lc->state, LIFECYCLE_UNINITIALIZED);
231 }




References atomic_store_u64(),
    LIFECYCLE_UNINITIALIZED, log_dev, and
    lifecycle_t::state.


Referenced by audio_stop_thread().






bool lifecycle_destroy_once (lifecycle_t * lc)


Lock-free concurrent destruction: CAS INITIALIZED →
    DESTROYING.




Winner receives true and must complete the two-phase sequence:



  
1.

  
Do actual destroy work

  
2.

  
Call lifecycle_destroy_commit() on completion




Losing callers return false and should skip cleanup work. Once
    destruction begins, no further operations are possible until the winner
    completes.




Parameters


lc lifecycle state


Returns


true if THIS caller won the destroy race and should do
  work



false if not INITIALIZED, already being destroyed, or in DEAD
    state




Safe for concurrent callers. Exactly one caller gets true (the CAS
    winner). Prevents double-join and other double-cleanup issues when multiple
    threads call cleanup functions concurrently.


Definition at line 181 of file lifecycle.c.


181                                              {
182   if (lc == NULL) {
183     log_dev("[lifecycle] destroy_once: NULL lifecycle pointer");
184     return false;
185   }
186 
187   uint64_t expected = LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZED;
188   if (!atomic_cas_u64(&lc->state, &expected, LIFECYCLE_DESTROYING)) {
189     // If not initialized, nothing to destroy
190     if (expected == LIFECYCLE_UNINITIALIZED) {
191       log_dev("[lifecycle] destroy_once: already uninitialized, nothing to destroy");
192       return false;
193     }
194 
195     // If dead, never allow destruction (already permanently shut down)
196     if (expected == LIFECYCLE_DEAD) {
197       log_dev("[lifecycle] destroy_once: module is dead, no destruction allowed");
198       return false;
199     }
200 
201     // If already destroying, don't duplicate work (return false, let first destroyer finish)
202     if (expected == LIFECYCLE_DESTROYING) {
203       log_dev("[lifecycle] destroy_once: already destroying, skipping (first destroyer has priority)");
204       return false;
205     }
206 
207     // If initializing, skip destruction (init may still be in progress)
208     if (expected == LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZING) {
209       log_dev("[lifecycle] destroy_once: still initializing, skipping destruction");
210       return false;
211     }
212 
213     // Unexpected state
214     log_dev("[lifecycle] destroy_once: unexpected state: %d", expected);
215     return false;
216   }
217 
218   // Winner: state is now LIFECYCLE_DESTROYING
219   // Caller must call lifecycle_destroy_commit()
220   log_dev("[lifecycle] destroy_once: won CAS, transitioned to DESTROYING");
221   return true;
222 }






References atomic_cas_u64(), LIFECYCLE_DEAD,
    LIFECYCLE_DESTROYING, LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZED,
    LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZING, LIFECYCLE_UNINITIALIZED,
    log_dev, and lifecycle_t::state.


Referenced by audio_stop_thread().






bool lifecycle_init (lifecycle_t * lc, const char *
  name)


CAS-based initialization: UNINIT → INITIALIZED.




Parameters


lc lifecycle state (may include sync_type and sync
  pointer)


name name tag for any sync primitive (for debugging), or NULL if no
  sync


Returns


true if THIS caller won the race and should do init work



false if already INITIALIZED, INITIALIZING, or DEAD




Suitable for single-threaded startup or contexts where the caller
    guarantees serialization. If lc->sync_type != SYNC_NONE, initializes the
    sync primitive.


Definition at line 15 of file lifecycle.c.


15                                                        {
16   if (lc == NULL) {
17     log_dev("[lifecycle] init: NULL lifecycle pointer");
18     return false;
19   }
20   uint64_t expected = LIFECYCLE_UNINITIALIZED;
21   if (!atomic_cas_u64(&lc->state, &expected, LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZED)) {
22     log_dev("[lifecycle] init: %s already initialized (current state: %llu)", name ? name : "<unnamed>", expected);
23     return false; // Already initialized or in INITIALIZING/DEAD state
24   }
25 
26   /* Winner: initialize sync primitive if configured */
27   if (lc->sync_type == LIFECYCLE_SYNC_MUTEX && lc->sync.mutex != NULL) {
28     log_dev("[lifecycle] init: %s initializing mutex", name ? name : "<unnamed>");
29     mutex_init(lc->sync.mutex, name);
30   } else if (lc->sync_type == LIFECYCLE_SYNC_RWLOCK && lc->sync.rwlock != NULL) {
31     log_dev("[lifecycle] init: %s initializing rwlock", name ? name : "<unnamed>");
32     rwlock_init(lc->sync.rwlock, name);
33   } else {
34     log_dev("[lifecycle] init: %s initialized (no sync primitive)", name ? name : "<unnamed>");
35   }
36 
37   return true;
38 }






References atomic_cas_u64(), LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZED,
    LIFECYCLE_SYNC_MUTEX, LIFECYCLE_SYNC_RWLOCK,
    LIFECYCLE_UNINITIALIZED, log_dev, lifecycle_t::mutex,
    mutex_init(), lifecycle_t::rwlock, rwlock_init(),
    lifecycle_t::state, lifecycle_t::sync, and
    lifecycle_t::sync_type.

    ansi_fast_init_256color(), asciichat_error_stats_get(),
    asciichat_error_stats_init(), asciichat_error_stats_record(),
    asciichat_pcre2_singleton_compile(),
    asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(), audio_sender_init(),
    audio_stop_thread(), buffer_pool_init_global(),
    client_crypto_init(), colorscheme_init(),
    init_default_luminance_palette(), log_init(),
    log_search_init(), named_init(), options_state_init(),
    server_main(), stats_init(), symbol_cache_init(), and
    timer_system_init().






void
  lifecycle_init_abort (lifecycle_t * lc)


Abort failed initialization: INITIALIZING → UNINITIALIZED.
    Call this if lifecycle_init_once() returns true but init work
  fails.




Parameters


lc lifecycle state


Allows the next caller to retry initialization. Wakes
  spinners.


Definition at line 87 of file lifecycle.c.


87                                            {
88   if (lc == NULL) {
89     log_dev("[lifecycle] init_abort: NULL lifecycle pointer");
90     return;
91   }
92   log_dev("[lifecycle] init_abort: transitioning INITIALIZING → UNINITIALIZED (retry allowed)");
93   atomic_store_u64(&lc->state, LIFECYCLE_UNINITIALIZED);
94 }






References atomic_store_u64(),
    LIFECYCLE_UNINITIALIZED, log_dev, and
    lifecycle_t::state.


Referenced by audio_client_init(), and
    symbol_cache_init().






void
  lifecycle_init_commit (lifecycle_t * lc)


Commit successful initialization: INITIALIZING →
    INITIALIZED. Call this after lifecycle_init_once() returns true and
    init work succeeds.




Parameters


lc lifecycle state


Wakes all spinners waiting in lifecycle_init_once(). Must
    be called by the lifecycle_init_once() winner after successful init
    work.


Definition at line 78 of file lifecycle.c.


78                                             {
79   if (lc == NULL) {
80     log_dev("[lifecycle] init_commit: NULL lifecycle pointer");
81     return;
82   }
83   log_dev("[lifecycle] init_commit: transitioning INITIALIZING → INITIALIZED");
84   atomic_store_u64(&lc->state, LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZED);
85 }






References atomic_store_u64(),
    LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZED, log_dev, and
  lifecycle_t::state.


Referenced by asciichat_instr_coverage_enabled(),
    asciichat_instr_runtime_get(),
    asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(), audio_client_init(),
    log_search_init(), named_init(), and
    splash_set_update_notification().






bool lifecycle_init_once (lifecycle_t * lc)


Lock-free concurrent initialization: CAS UNINIT →
    INITIALIZING.




Winner receives true and must complete the two-phase sequence:



  
1.

  
Do actual init work

  
2.

  
Call lifecycle_init_commit() on success or
      lifecycle_init_abort() on failure




Losing callers spin on the transient INITIALIZING state until the
    winner commits, then return false without doing work.




Parameters


lc lifecycle state


Returns


true if THIS caller won the init race and should do work



false if init already happened (or is in progress) or module is
    DEAD




Safe for concurrent callers. Exactly one caller gets true (the CAS
    winner). Other concurrent callers spin until the winner moves to INITIALIZED
    or DEAD.


Definition at line 40 of file lifecycle.c.


40                                           {
41   if (lc == NULL) {
42     log_dev("[lifecycle] init_once: NULL lifecycle pointer");
43     return false;
44   }
45 
46   uint64_t expected = LIFECYCLE_UNINITIALIZED;
47   if (!atomic_cas_u64(&lc->state, &expected, LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZING)) {
48     // If already initialized, just return false (no work needed)
49     if (expected == LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZED) {
50       log_dev("[lifecycle] init_once: already initialized");
51       return false;
52     }
53 
54     // If dead, never allow re-init
55     if (expected == LIFECYCLE_DEAD) {
56       log_dev("[lifecycle] init_once: module is dead, no re-init allowed");
57       return false;
58     }
59 
60     // If initializing, don't spin (caller may be retrying or it may complete asynchronously)
61     // Just return false to indicate this thread doesn't need to do init work
62     if (expected == LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZING) {
63       log_dev("[lifecycle] init_once: already initializing, skipping (will resolve asynchronously)");
64       return false;
65     }
66 
67     // Unexpected state
68     log_dev("[lifecycle] init_once: unexpected state: %d", expected);
69     return false;
70   }
71 
72   // Winner: state is now LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZING
73   // Caller must call lifecycle_init_commit() or lifecycle_init_abort()
74   log_dev("[lifecycle] init_once: won CAS, transitioned to INITIALIZING");
75   return true;
76 }






References atomic_cas_u64(), LIFECYCLE_DEAD,
    LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZED, LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZING,
    LIFECYCLE_UNINITIALIZED, log_dev, and
    lifecycle_t::state.


Referenced by asciichat_instr_coverage_enabled(),
    asciichat_instr_runtime_get(), audio_client_init(),
    log_search_init(), and splash_set_update_notification().






bool lifecycle_is_dead (const lifecycle_t *
  lc)


Query: is module in DEAD state?




Parameters


lc lifecycle state


Returns


true if state == LIFECYCLE_DEAD


Load-only, no side effects. Safe to call from any thread.


Definition at line 149 of file lifecycle.c.


149                                               {
150   if (lc == NULL)
151     return false;
152   return atomic_load_u64(&lc->state) == LIFECYCLE_DEAD;
153 }






References atomic_load_u64(), LIFECYCLE_DEAD, and
    lifecycle_t::state.






bool lifecycle_is_initialized (const lifecycle_t *
  lc)


Query: is module in INITIALIZED state?




Parameters


lc lifecycle state


Returns


true if state == LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZED


Load-only, no side effects. Safe to call from any thread.


Definition at line 143 of file lifecycle.c.


143                                                      {
144   if (lc == NULL)
145     return false;
146   return atomic_load_u64(&lc->state) == LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZED;
147 }

    and lifecycle_t::state.

bool lifecycle_reset (lifecycle_t * lc)


Reset initialized module: INITIALIZED → UNINITIALIZED.




Parameters


lc lifecycle state (may include sync_type and sync
  pointer)


Returns


true if THIS caller should do reset work



false if not INITIALIZED or in DEAD state




Allows re-initialization after reset (like shutdown, but keeps
    sync primitives). If lc->sync_type != SYNC_NONE, destroys the sync
    primitive (will be recreated on next init). Used for modules that support
    reset/reinit cycles (e.g., client crypto reconnect).


Definition at line 155 of file lifecycle.c.


155                                       {
156   if (lc == NULL) {
157     log_dev("[lifecycle] reset: NULL lifecycle pointer");
158     return false;
159   }
160 
161   uint64_t expected = LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZED;
162   if (!atomic_cas_u64(&lc->state, &expected, LIFECYCLE_UNINITIALIZED)) {
163     log_dev("[lifecycle] reset: not in INITIALIZED state (current: %d)", expected);
164     return false; // Not in INITIALIZED state
165   }
166 
167   /* Winner: destroy sync primitive if configured (reset keeps same primitive) */
168   if (lc->sync_type == LIFECYCLE_SYNC_MUTEX && lc->sync.mutex != NULL) {
169     log_dev("[lifecycle] reset: destroying and resetting mutex");
170     mutex_destroy(lc->sync.mutex);
171   } else if (lc->sync_type == LIFECYCLE_SYNC_RWLOCK && lc->sync.rwlock != NULL) {
172     log_dev("[lifecycle] reset: destroying and resetting rwlock");
173     rwlock_destroy(lc->sync.rwlock);
174   } else {
175     log_dev("[lifecycle] reset: completed (no sync primitive, allows reinit)");
176   }
177 
178   return true;
179 }






References atomic_cas_u64(), LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZED,
    LIFECYCLE_SYNC_MUTEX, LIFECYCLE_SYNC_RWLOCK,
    LIFECYCLE_UNINITIALIZED, log_dev, lifecycle_t::mutex,
    mutex_destroy(), lifecycle_t::rwlock, rwlock_destroy(),
    lifecycle_t::state, lifecycle_t::sync, and
    lifecycle_t::sync_type.


Referenced by client_crypto_init().






bool lifecycle_shutdown (lifecycle_t * lc)


Regular shutdown: INITIALIZED → UNINITIALIZED.




Parameters


lc lifecycle state (may include sync_type and sync
  pointer)


Returns


true if THIS caller should do shutdown work



false if already UNINITIALIZED or DEAD




Allows re-initialization after shutdown (unlike shutdown_forever).
    If lc->sync_type != SYNC_NONE, destroys the sync primitive.


Definition at line 96 of file lifecycle.c.


96                                          {
97   if (lc == NULL) {
98     log_dev("[lifecycle] shutdown: NULL lifecycle pointer");
99     return false;
100   }
101   uint64_t expected = LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZED;
102   if (!atomic_cas_u64(&lc->state, &expected, LIFECYCLE_UNINITIALIZED)) {
103     log_dev("[lifecycle] shutdown: not in INITIALIZED state (current: %d)", expected);
104     return false; // Not initialized or in unexpected state
105   }
106 
107   /* Winner: destroy sync primitive if configured */
108   if (lc->sync_type == LIFECYCLE_SYNC_MUTEX && lc->sync.mutex != NULL) {
109     log_dev("[lifecycle] shutdown: destroying mutex");
110     mutex_destroy(lc->sync.mutex);
111   } else if (lc->sync_type == LIFECYCLE_SYNC_RWLOCK && lc->sync.rwlock != NULL) {
112     log_dev("[lifecycle] shutdown: destroying rwlock");
113     rwlock_destroy(lc->sync.rwlock);
114   } else {
115     log_dev("[lifecycle] shutdown: completed (no sync primitive)");
116   }
117 
118   return true;
119 }






References atomic_cas_u64(), LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZED,
    LIFECYCLE_SYNC_MUTEX, LIFECYCLE_SYNC_RWLOCK,
    LIFECYCLE_UNINITIALIZED, log_dev, lifecycle_t::mutex,
    mutex_destroy(), lifecycle_t::rwlock, rwlock_destroy(),
    lifecycle_t::state, lifecycle_t::sync, and
    lifecycle_t::sync_type.


Referenced by acds_strings_destroy(),
    asciichat_instr_runtime_global_destroy(),
    asciichat_pcre2_cleanup_all(), audio_sender_cleanup(),
    audio_terminate_portaudio_final(),
    buffer_pool_cleanup_global(), colorscheme_destroy(),
    crypto_client_cleanup(), log_destroy(),
    log_search_destroy(), named_destroy(), named_init(),
    options_state_destroy(), options_state_init(),
    platform_cleanup_binary_path_cache(),
    simd_caches_destroy_all(), stats_cleanup(),
    symbol_cache_destroy(), and timer_system_destroy().






bool lifecycle_shutdown_forever (lifecycle_t *
  lc)


Permanent shutdown: any non-DEAD → DEAD.




Parameters


lc lifecycle state


Returns


true if module was INITIALIZED and caller should do
  shutdown work



false if already DEAD




Once DEAD, future init/init_once calls always return false. Spins
    if called while init_once is in progress (INITIALIZING state). Used for
    modules that must never be re-initialized.


Definition at line 121 of file lifecycle.c.


121                                                  {
122   if (lc == NULL) {
123     log_dev("[lifecycle] shutdown_forever: NULL lifecycle pointer");
124     return false;
125   }
126 
127   uint64_t current;
128   do {
129     current = atomic_load_u64(&lc->state);
130     if (current == LIFECYCLE_DEAD) {
131       log_dev("[lifecycle] shutdown_forever: already DEAD");
132       return false;
133     }
134     if (current == LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZING) {
135       log_dev("[lifecycle] shutdown_forever: spinning on INITIALIZING");
136     }
137   } while (current == LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZING || !atomic_cas_u64(&lc->state, &current, LIFECYCLE_DEAD));
138 
139   log_dev("[lifecycle] shutdown_forever: transitioned to DEAD (was in state: %d)", current);
140   return current == LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZED;
141 }






References atomic_cas_u64(), atomic_load_u64(),
    LIFECYCLE_DEAD, LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZED,
    LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZING, log_dev, and
    lifecycle_t::state.
